ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Nyɔgɩmaa pʋna gannɩ rɛ Gyɔɔŋ fa kɩ laalɩ, aŋ aa kpa tɛpɩɩŋbii vʋwa ʋ tɩŋa. Nyɩŋgɩrɩgɩsɩ abee tʋʋ rɛ fa yaa ʋ kɩdiiliye.
translated: The Lord has given us a new song to sing, a song of praise. Let us sing it with joy and gladness. May our voices resound with His glory.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Saŋa no tɩyaŋ baala balɩya sɩ kɩ pala baga tɩyaŋ dɩ Wɩɩsɩ ko kpa kɩdɩgɩ aŋ leŋ kɩdɩgɩ ta.
translated: If you want to be a good person, you must be humble and respect the elders.

ref: But he didn't have any money to pay what he owed. The king ordered him to be sold, along with his wife and children and all he owned, in order to pay the debt.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ tʋŋtʋnnɩ hʋ bɩ kaŋ molbii ʋ aa sɩ tuŋ. Nyɛ rɛ ʋ nɩhɩyawʋ hʋ baa dɩ ba kaŋ ʋ bee ʋ haaŋ abee ʋ balɩya abee kɩŋ buloŋ ʋ aa kana, a kaŋ mʋ yallɩ a ko tuŋ kyɩŋ hʋ.
translated: You are the one who will bring me joy. I will be your joy, your happiness, your everything. I will be your light, your strength, your everything. I will be your love, your hope, your everything.

ref: As soon as Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went home with Simon and Andrew.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii lɩɩ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ haŋ lagɩ lagɩ hʋ, ʋ bee Gyemsi abee Gyɔɔŋ, a kpa mʋ Siimɔŋ abee Aŋduru dɩya.
translated: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu sii lɩɩ wɩɩkyʋwaldɩya hʋ tɩyaŋ haŋ lagɩ lagɩ hʋ, ʋ bee Gyemsi abee Gyɔɔŋ, a kpa mʋ Siimɔŋ abee Aŋduru dɩya.

ref: No one patches old clothes by sewing on a piece of new cloth. The new piece would shrink and tear a bigger hole.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ bɩl basɩ tɩya ba a baa, “Nal buloŋ bɩ sɩ kpa taŋfalɩɩ a ala tɔ gabɩnɩɩ bol? Dɩ ɩ rɛ yaa gɛɛ, taŋfalɩɩ hʋ sɩ kʋʋrɩ, dɩ gabɩnɩɩ hʋ marɩ kɩyasɩ pɛ.
translated: Are you the one who said that we should go to the market today? Did you say that we should go to the market to buy something? I don't know, maybe we should go to the market to buy something.

ref: Even the hairs on your head are counted.
text: Wɩɩsɩ paalɩ gyɩŋ ma kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ nyupʋna nyʋwa rɛ.
translated: The wind is blowing gently today.

ref: The chief priests and the whole council wanted to put Jesus to death. So they tried to find some people who would tell lies about him in court.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Gyuuma wɩɩkyʋwal sɩlaala nɩhɩyasɩ abee nala hʋ buloŋ aa laŋŋɩ kɩ vʋʋlɩ wɩya, kɩ kyɛ dɩ ba nyɩya wɩya a tɩya Yesu dɩ ba kpʋ ʋ.
translated: The Lord Jesus Christ is the only way to salvation, he is the truth and the life. Whoever believes in him will not perish but have eternal life.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Dɩ nal lɛ fɩla ɩ dɩ ɩ kyʋŋ ʋ kyʋgɩsɩ a vala meeldaaŋ, leŋ dɩ ma bɩl vala meeldaaŋ a pɛ.
translated: I will go to the market to buy something, I will buy something from the market.

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: Yesu aa nɩɩ wɩya no, ʋ nyʋwa fɩyɛlɩ. Ɛɛ rɛ ʋ basɩ tɩya nɩgyamaa hʋ fa aa tɩŋa ʋ hal a baa, “Wɩtɩɩ rɛ ŋ basɩ kɩ tɩya ma, ŋ ha bɩ maakyiye na laadii no iriŋ Iziral paalʋʋ nal kɩdɩgɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ.
translated: Jesus said to them, "I tell you the truth, unless you are born again, you cannot see the kingdom of God.  You must be born of water and the Spirit to enter the kingdom of God. For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You give God a tenth of the spices from your garden, such as mint, dill, and cumin. Yet you neglect the more important matters of the Law, such as justice, mercy, and faithfulness. These are the important things you should have done, though you should not have left the others undone either.
text: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Paaponni hʋ buloŋ ma aa kpa we kɩdiiliye tɩyaŋ ba sʋwala sʋma, ma aa kaŋ ba kpaa leye fi fi rɛ, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ. Ma aa yaa wɩmʋlla no buloŋ nɛ aŋ vɩya dɩ ma tɩŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩya hʋ Moosi fa aa biŋ tɩya ma anɩɩ ma kaŋ wɩtɩɩ wɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, a kɩ fá nala sikii, a kɩ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ abee wɩtɩɩ. Ma aa kaŋ ma kɩna kpaa leye fi fi, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ gɛɛ, ʋ yaa wɩwelii rɛ, amɛ ɛɛ bɩ daga dɩ ma vɩya wɩkaalɩya hʋ kɛ ta.
translated: Mamaa Wɩɩsɩ Teŋ dɩdagɩla abee Farasiima, ɛɛdɔŋ minaafigi tɩmma! Ma sɩ na tʋwara kɩŋkaŋ. Paaponni hʋ buloŋ ma aa kpa we kɩdiiliye tɩyaŋ ba sʋwala sʋma, ma aa kaŋ ba kpaa leye fi fi rɛ, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ. Ma aa yaa wɩmʋlla no buloŋ nɛ aŋ vɩya dɩ ma tɩŋ sɩfɩyaŋ wɩya hʋ Moosi fa aa biŋ tɩya ma anɩɩ ma kaŋ wɩtɩɩ wɩɩ buloŋ tɩyaŋ, a kɩ fá nala sikii, a kɩ tɩŋ Wɩɩsɩ abee wɩtɩɩ. Ma aa kaŋ ma kɩna kpaa leye fi fi, a kpa lɩdɩgɩ tɩya Wɩɩsɩ gɛɛ, ʋ yaa wɩwelii rɛ, amɛ ɛɛ bɩ daga dɩ ma vɩya wɩkaalɩya hʋ kɛ ta.

ref: John the Baptist did not go around eating and drinking, and you said, “That man has a demon in him!”
text: Ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ yaŋ, saŋa hʋ tɩyaŋ Gyɔɔŋ gyɩ aa ko ma lee, ʋ gyɩ aa vʋwa nyʋwa rɛ, aŋ bee nyʋwa sɩŋ mɛ. Ɛɛ rɛ nala baa dɩ gyɩŋbɔŋ nɛ kana ʋ.
translated: If you want to see, let me show you, I will show you the way of the world, and you will see how the world works. If you want to know, let me tell you.

ref: But they did not sleep together before her baby was born. Then Joseph named him Jesus.
text: Amɛ ʋ beel-ʋ ha gyɩ bɩ gyɩŋ dɔmɔŋ anɩɩ baal bee haaŋ aŋka Mɛɛrɩ lʋl boyoŋbiye. Nyɛ rɛ Gyosɛfʋ we biye hʋ feŋ Yesu.
translated: And the angel appeared to them, saying, "Do not be afraid, for behold, I bring you good tidings of great joy which will be to all the people. For there is born to you this day in the city of David a Savior, who is Christ the Lord."

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ ka Siimɔŋ bee ʋ kyaŋsɩ hʋ mɛ sii ko a kɩ kyɛ ʋ.
translated: If you see Simon, tell him that I said hello to him.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Ɛɛ rɛ porikudaala hʋ fá mʋ heeli tɩya bagɩdaala abee tɔɔ hʋ nala buloŋ. Ba buloŋ sii ko lɩɩ Yesu lee.
translated: The one who does not love does not know God, because God is love. Whoever does not love does not know God.

ref: The Son of Man did not come to be a slave master, but a slave who will give his life to rescue many people. Jesus Heals Two Blind Men (Mark
text: anɩɩ mɩyaŋ Nihuwobiŋ Biye aa kɩɩ gɛɛ. Ŋ bɩ ko dɩ nala tʋma kɩ tɩya ŋ, amɛ nala rɛ ŋ ko dɩ ŋ tʋma tɩya, aŋ kpa ŋ mɩɩbol mɛ lenni sʋʋ a laa nala yʋga ta ba wɩbɔmɔ tɩyaŋ.” Yesu suro nyʋlɩma balɩya sɩya Maakɩ
translated: Then Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, unless you eat the flesh of the Son of Man and drink his blood, you have no life in you. Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood has eternal life, and I will raise him up at the last day. For my flesh is real food and my blood is real drink. Whoever eats my flesh and drinks my blood remains in me, and I in him. Just as the living Father sent me and I live because of the Father, so the one who eats me will live because of me. This is the bread that came down from heaven. Unlike your ancestors who ate manna and still died, whoever eats this bread will live forever.”

ref: The shadow of a cloud passed over and covered them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my Son, and I love him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ taalbal kɩdɩgɩ ko tɔ ba, ka taal kɩdɩgɩ lɩɩ taalbal hʋ tɩyaŋ a baa, “Ŋ biikyoolii rɛ nyɛ. Ma gyegile kɩ nɩɩ ʋ wɩbasɩɩ.”
translated: The man said to the child, "Go and bring me water. I am very thirsty."

ref: Jesus answered, “Go away Satan! The Scriptures say: ‘Worship the Lord your God and serve only him.’ ”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ Yesu basɩ tɩya ʋ a baa, “Sɩtaanɩ, fasɩ mʋ ŋ sɩyaŋ! Wɩɩsɩ teŋ tɩyaŋ ba saba rɛ a baa, Kɩ kyʋwalɩ ŋ Tɩɩna Wɩɩsɩ dʋŋ abee gyɩrɩma aŋ kɩ tɩŋa ʋ.”
translated: Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted by the devil. After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.

ref: The crowd told them to be quiet, but they shouted even louder, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Ɛɛ rɛ nɩgyamaa hʋ nɔsɩ ba dɩ ba leŋ ba kyagɩŋsɩ hʋ. Amɛ ba marɩ yɩrɩ te gɛɛ a baa, “Á nɩhɩyawʋ, Deviti nihii, fá á sikii.”
translated: We are going to tell you a story. Listen carefully. It is a story about a woman named Deviti. She was very beautiful.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Beewɩya ɛɛ hʋ tɩɩ ɩ aa yaŋ di ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna sarɩya, ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ mɛ sɩ di ɩ sarɩya. Ɩ aa yaŋ kpa gɛɛ tɩŋ ɩ dɔŋtɩɩna, ɛɛ tɩɩ rɛ Wɩɩsɩ mɛ sɩ kpa tɩŋa ɩ.
translated: If you see a person who is walking in the direction of the market, tell him that you will meet him there. If you see a person who is walking in the direction of the market, tell him that you will meet him there.

ref: Don't his sisters still live here in our town? How can he do all this?”
text: Á bee ʋ naatoliye buloŋ daa we daha nyɛ? Lee tɩyaŋ nɛ ʋ yaŋ na wɩya no buloŋ?”
translated: Are you not going to tell me what happened?  Did you not see what happened at the place?

ref: While Peter was still speaking, the shadow of a bright cloud passed over them. From the cloud a voice said, “This is my own dear Son, and I am pleased with him. Listen to what he says!”
text: Piita ha fa aa basɩ wɩya hʋ gɛɛ dɩ taal tʋlɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ ko tuu tɔ ba, ka taal lɩɩ taal tʋlɩɩ hʋ tɩyaŋ a baa, “Ŋ Biikyoolii rɛ nyɛ. Ŋ teŋ fɩyɛla rɛ ʋ tɩyaŋ. Ma gyegili kɩ nɩɩ ʋ nyʋwa.”
translated: Peter said to them, "Truly, I say to you, this is not the way to do it. You are looking for something else. You are looking for something that is not here. I will not be your guide."

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Dɩ poriku-gyamaa mɛ sɩŋ kpaga doŋ a kɩ di kɩna.
translated: I will go to the market to buy something.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Koo dɩ ʋ baa ɩ tɩya ʋ fuwonamɩya, ɩ sɩ kpa dʋŋ tɩya ʋ? Aayɩ, ɩ bɩ sɩ yaa gɛɛ!
translated: Where are you going? I am going to the market.

ref: Then Joseph put the body in his own tomb that had been cut into solid rock and had never been used. He rolled a big stone against the entrance to the tomb and went away.
text: a kpa ʋ hogo ʋ tɩɩ bʋʋfalɩɩ kɩdɩgɩ ʋ fa aa lurigo bʋyɩ tɩyaŋ a biliŋsi bʋbal a ko tɔ bʋwa hʋ nyʋwa aŋ mʋ.
translated: If you want to eat, you must first go and fetch water to cook.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Ba aa ko lusi ba wɩbɔmɔ aŋ tuu Gyɔɔdaŋ fuwo hʋ, ʋ fo ba wɩɩkyʋwalnɩɩ.
translated: If you want to know the truth about God, you must first seek him.
